amendments.body: In `extract_codex_usage_from_stdout` (process_backend.rs:1300-1310), the `.as_u64().map(|v| v as u32)` pattern silently truncates values exceeding u32::MAX. Use `u32::try_from(v).ok()` instead of `v as u32` to match the safety guarantees of the Claude path, which uses serde's native u32 deserialization (and would reject overflow). Apply the same fix to all three token field extractions in this function.
amendments.rationale: The Claude CLI path deserializes directly into Option<u32> via serde, which rejects values > u32::MAX. The Codex path goes through as_u64() then `as u32`, which silently wraps. While token counts this large are unrealistic, the inconsistency creates a latent correctness gap between the two backends. Using try_from is a one-line fix per field with zero performance cost.

amendments.body: In both Claude usage extraction (process_backend.rs:186) and Codex usage extraction (process_backend.rs:1312), `Some(p + c)` can panic in debug builds or wrap in release builds if prompt_tokens + completion_tokens exceeds u32::MAX. Use `p.checked_add(c)` instead of `Some(p + c)` for the total_tokens computation.
amendments.rationale: While u32 overflow is extremely unlikely for realistic token counts, checked_add is a zero-cost safeguard that converts overflow to None (no total reported) rather than a panic or incorrect value. This is consistent with the Option<u32> semantics already used for all other token count fields.

payload.summary: Reviewed all changes across 5 files (model.rs, process_backend.rs, openrouter_backend.rs, stub_backend.rs, service.rs). The implementation is well-structured and correct: TokenCounts is properly extended, all construction sites are updated, backward compatibility is preserved via serde(default) and Default derive, new tests cover key scenarios, all 667 tests pass, and clippy is clean. Two minor correctness issues found: potential u32 arithmetic overflow in total_tokens computation, and silent u64→u32 truncation in the Codex extraction path.
